message:
chore: update CLI robot module and refresh beads issue tracker

Minor updates to the CLI robot.rs module and sync .beads/issues.jsonl with
current issue status.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -611,7 +611,7 @@
 {"id":"br-2bbt.11.1","title":"Snapshot tests for all new widgets (palette, toast, modal, action menu, charts)","description":"Create snapshot test infrastructure and golden files for every new TUI widget:

## Setup
1. Use ftui_harness to create deterministic rendering environment
2. Fixed terminal size per test (80x24 for Compact, 160x48 for Large)
3. Golden file format: .expected.txt (rendered buffer as text)
4. Comparison: exact character match with diff on failure

## Snapshot Tests Required

### Command Palette (br-2bbt.1)
- palette_empty_large.expected.txt: empty query, all entries visible
- palette_filtered_large.expected.txt: query "msg", filtered results
- palette_compact.expected.txt: palette in 80x24 terminal
- palette_with_entity_results.expected.txt: agent/thread entries shown

### Toast Notifications (br-2bbt.2)
- toast_single_info.expected.txt: one info toast, top-right
- toast_triple_mixed.expected.txt: 3 stacked (info, warning, error)
- toast_compact.expected.txt: toast in 80x24 terminal
- toast_with_action.expected.txt: toast with action button

### Modal Dialogs (br-2bbt.5)
- modal_confirmation_large.expected.txt: centered confirm dialog
- modal_two_actions.expected.txt: confirm + cancel buttons
- modal_compact.expected.txt: modal in 80x24 terminal

### Action Menu (br-2bbt.5)
- action_menu_messages.expected.txt: message actions list
- action_menu_reservations.expected.txt: reservation actions list

### Native Charts (br-2bbt.4)
- sparkline_20points.expected.txt: sparkline with 20 data points
- progress_bar_80pct.expected.txt: gauge at 80%
- progress_bar_15pct.expected.txt: gauge at 15% (red threshold)
- percentile_ribbon.expected.txt: p50/p95/p99 chart

## Test Pattern
Each test follows:
1. Create widget with fixed test data
2. Render into ftui_harness buffer at fixed size
3. Compare with golden file
4. On mismatch: print diff, write actual to .actual.txt for review
5. Test name includes terminal size for clarity

## Acceptance Criteria
- Minimum 15 snapshot tests covering all new widgets
- Golden files checked into repo under tests/tui_snapshots/
- cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-server runs all snapshot tests
- Clear diff output on snapshot mismatch
- Regeneration command documented (e.g., UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 cargo test)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:11:33.085028198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T09:50:54.700815779Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T09:50:54.700788708Z","close_reason":"Implemented snapshot tests for TUI V2 widgets using full-app rendering approach. Added command palette snapshots (80x24, 160x48), reservations/tool_metrics screens at multiple sizes, and large terminal variants for dashboard/messages/search. Uses ftui_harness assert_snapshot! macro with BLESS=1 for baseline creation.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["snapshots","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.1","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:07.413031391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.1","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:11:33.085028198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.1","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:07.600874547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.1","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:07.791550579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.1","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:07.979500645Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.11.2","title":"E2E test script: tests/e2e/test_tui_v2.sh with comprehensive logging","description":"Create comprehensive E2E test script for TUI V2 features:

## Script: tests/e2e/test_tui_v2.sh

Uses e2e_lib.sh harness for assertion macros and logging.

### Test Cases (minimum 30 assertions)

1. test_command_palette_entity_search (4 assertions):
   - Start server, register agent, send message
   - Verify palette shows agent in entity results (via MCP tool call + state inspection)
   - Verify palette shows message subject in entity results
   - Verify palette shows thread in entity results
   - Assert palette entry count > static count (proves dynamic entries loaded)

2. test_toast_on_message (4 assertions):
   - Register 2 agents, send message from A to B
   - Assert toast notification created (inspect notification queue state via MCP)
   - Assert toast contains sender name and subject
   - Assert toast severity is Info
   - Wait 6 seconds, assert toast auto-dismissed

3. test_toast_on_tool_error (3 assertions):
   - Trigger a tool call that produces an error (e.g., send_message with invalid project)
   - Assert error toast created with tool name
   - Assert toast severity is Error

4. test_reservation_expiry_warning (3 assertions):
   - Create reservation with short TTL (10s)
   - Wait until within 5 min of expiry
   - Assert warning toast generated for expiring reservation

5. test_virtualized_timeline (4 assertions):
   - Send 500 rapid messages (generates 1000+ events: MessageSent + MessageReceived)
   - Verify event ring buffer has >1000 events
   - Verify server is still responsive (health_check succeeds)
   - Verify no frame render errors in server log

6. test_modal_confirmation (4 assertions):
   - Create file reservation
   - Trigger force_release_file_reservation via MCP
   - Verify reservation was released
   - Verify server processed the action (no modal in headless mode, but confirm the
     modal infrastructure compiles and initializes correctly via unit tests)

7. test_action_menu_actions (4 assertions):
   - Send message, verify it appears in fetch_inbox
   - Verify per-screen action sets are defined (test via internal API/module test)
   - Verify action menu has correct actions for Messages screen
   - Verify action menu has correct actions for Reservations screen

8. test_global_inbox (3 assertions):
   - Create 2 projects with messages in each
   - Fetch inbox globally (if Global mode is API-accessible)
   - Assert messages from both projects returned
   - Assert project context included in results

9. test_search_field_scope (3 assertions):
   - Create messages with distinct subject and body content
   - Search with default field scope, verify results
   - Search with subject-only field scope, verify body-only matches excluded

10. test_notification_queue_config (3 assertions):
    - Set AM_TUI_TOAST_SEVERITY=error
    - Generate Info-level event (message received)
    - Assert Info toast was NOT created (filtered by severity threshold)

### Testing Mechanism

TUI E2E tests work through the MCP stdio/HTTP transport to set up data and verify state.
For TUI-specific rendering tests (palette overlay, toast visibility), use snapshot tests
(br-2bbt.11.1) rather than E2E since E2E can only verify server state, not rendered pixels.

For data-driven tests (global inbox, search scope, notification config), HTTP transport
with JSON assertions suffices.

### Logging Requirements
- Each assertion logs: test name, description, expected, actual, pass/fail
- Timing logged per test case
- Server logs captured to test_tui_v2.server.log
- On failure: dump last 50 lines of server log
- Exit summary: total assertions, passed, failed, duration

### Acceptance Criteria
- Minimum 30 assertions across 10+ test cases
- All tests pass with clean server startup/shutdown
- Uses e2e_lib.sh assert_eq, assert_contains, assert_not_empty macros
- Script exits 0 on all pass, 1 on any failure","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:11:33.305650387Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:32:15.615403796Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T15:32:15.615380002Z","close_reason":"scripts/e2e_tui_v2.sh completed with 10 test cases, 35+ assertions covering all TUI V2 features. Test infrastructure integrates with e2e_lib.sh, saves request/response artifacts, and handles FTS/timeout edge cases gracefully.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:08.168427697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:11:33.305650387Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:08.357200371Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:08.547171946Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:08.737747911Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":410,"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Codex update: I hardened scripts/e2e_tui_v2.sh MCP call plumbing to use e2e_lib.sh e2e_rpc_call artifact capture path (request/response/headers/timing/curl stderr per call), added retry handling for -32004 timeout responses, and increased server timeout window from 120s to 300s to reduce mid-suite server expiry. Also fixed generated RPC case IDs so each call gets a unique artifact directory instead of collisions.","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:20:12Z"},{"id":415,"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"E2E test script scripts/e2e_tui_v2.sh is fully implemented with 10 test cases, 35+ assertions. All TUI V2 features covered including command palette, toasts, virtualized timeline, modals, action menus, global inbox, search scope, and reservations. Tests pass when server is healthy. Known limitations: FTS5 needs init on fresh DB, 300s timeout for stress tests. Contact policies auto-set to open for reliable testing.","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:32:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.11.3","title":"Unit tests for all new TUI modules with coverage tracking","description":"Create unit tests for every new/enhanced module in the TUI V2 upgrade:

## IMPORTANT: File Location Guidance

Per AGENTS.md No File Proliferation, tests go in existing files #[cfg(test)] modules:
- Palette enhancements in tui_app.rs -> tests in tui_app.rs
- Toast mapping in tui_app.rs -> tests in tui_app.rs
- DataProvider implementations in respective screen files -> tests there
- ModalManager if in tui_app.rs -> tests there

Current TUI files: tui_app.rs (106KB), tui_bridge.rs, tui_chrome.rs, tui_events.rs (95KB),
tui_keymap.rs, tui_layout.rs (76KB), tui_macro.rs, tui_markdown.rs, tui_persist.rs (36KB),
tui_poller.rs, tui_preset.rs, tui_theme.rs, tui_widgets.rs (107KB)

## Test Coverage Targets Per Feature

### Command Palette Enhancement
- HintRanker wiring: record_usage increments, rank() returns new order
- HintRanker persistence: save/load round-trip via tui_persist
- Message entity entries: generated from DB with subjects as titles
- focused_event() audit: verify all screens that should return entities do so
- All 14 MailScreenId variants have palette navigation entries
Target: 8+ tests

### Toast Notification Enhancement
- Event-to-toast mapping for each ACTUAL event variant:
  - MessageReceived -> Info toast with sender + subject
  - ToolCallEnd (slow) -> Warning toast with tool name + duration
  - ToolCallEnd (error in preview) -> Error toast
  - AgentRegistered -> Info toast
  - ReservationGranted (exclusive) -> Info toast
  - ServerShutdown -> Error toast
- Events that produce NO toast: ToolCallStart, MessageSent, HttpRequest, HealthPulse
- Reservation expiry tracking: insert on grant, remove on release, warn at 5 min
- Severity threshold filtering (configurable via env var)
- Ctrl+T focus/unfocus state transitions
Target: 14+ tests

### Virtualization
- DataProvider.total_count() matches source
- DataProvider.window() returns correct slice
- DataProvider.window() out of bounds returns empty
- TimelineDataProvider wraps ring buffer correctly (non-blocking access)
- MessageDataProvider page caching (load, hit, evict)
- Prefetch trigger at edge proximity
- Invalidation clears cache
- RenderItem implementations produce expected output
Target: 10+ tests

### Modal Dialogs
- ModalManager show/close lifecycle
- Focus trapping: key events not passed to parent
- Enter executes selected action, Esc cancels
- Tab cycles action selection
Target: 6+ tests

### Action Menus
- Per-screen action sets: Messages, Reservations, Agents, Threads, Timeline, Contacts
- Navigation: Down/Up moves selection, wraps around
- First-letter jump
- Execute returns selected action
Target: 6+ tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- 44+ unit tests across all enhanced TUI modules
- cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-server runs all TUI unit tests
- All tests deterministic (no race conditions, no timing dependencies)","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Unit tests are implemented for all newly introduced TUI V2 modules and shared adapters
- [ ] Coverage tracking is enforced with module-level thresholds and failure gates
- [ ] Integration/E2E spot checks validate high-risk user flows represented by those modules
- [ ] Test output includes detailed diagnostics for failed assertions, snapshots, timings, and repro commands","notes":"2026-02-15 PeachKnoll progress: Added app-level tests in tui_app.rs for event-driven Ctrl+T toast focus toggling/navigation, reservation tracker grant/release processing on Tick, and ModalManager lifecycle/focus-trap callbacks (Enter/Escape/Tab/dismiss + global shortcut trap). Running targeted remote verification via rch build id 1957 (modal_manager_ filter); waiting for completion output.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildSpring","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:11:33.665906591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T05:04:49.382811891Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["testing","tui","unit-tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.3","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:08.924259948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.3","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:11:33.665906591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.3","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:09.113431538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.3","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:09.298381555Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.3","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:09.491544266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.3","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:09.684660982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":414,"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress: Fixed API compatibility issues in tui_app.rs test code.  {"id":"br-2bbt.11.4","title":"Performance benchmark tests for TUI V2 render paths","description":"Create performance benchmarks to enforce render time budgets:

## Benchmarks (in benches/ or tests/)

1. bench_virtualized_timeline_10k:
   - Create TimelineDataProvider with 10,000 events in ring buffer
   - Render 50 visible rows via VirtualizedList::new(&provider.window(0, 50))
   - Assert: p95 < 5ms per frame
   - Measure: average frame time, p50, p95, p99

2. bench_virtualized_messages_5k:
   - Create MessageDataProvider with 5,000 messages (DB-backed)
   - Render 40 visible rows with markdown preview
   - Assert: p95 < 8ms per frame

3. bench_command_palette_fuzzy_1k:
   - Create CommandRegistry with 1,000 entries
   - Run fuzzy match for 10 different queries
   - Assert: p95 < 2ms per search
   - Measure: entries scanned, results returned, time

4. bench_toast_overlay_render:
   - Render 3 stacked toasts over a full Dashboard screen
   - Assert: overlay adds < 1ms to frame time

5. bench_sparkline_100points:
   - Render sparkline with 100 data points
   - Assert: < 0.5ms per render

6. bench_modal_overlay_render:
   - Render confirmation modal over Messages screen
   - Assert: modal adds < 1ms to frame time

## Implementation
- Use std::time::Instant for measurement (not criterion, to avoid dep)
- Run each benchmark 100 iterations, compute percentiles
- Log results to stdout in structured format
- CI can parse and trend over time

## Acceptance Criteria
- All benchmarks within stated budgets
- Structured output for CI parsing
- No benchmark flakes (stable within 2x across runs)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:11:33.987449878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:35:30.736503770Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T15:35:30.736481197Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmarks","performance","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.4","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:11:33.987449878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.4","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:09.871346323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.4","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:10.060065807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":418,"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented 4 overlay/widget benchmarks in tui_app.rs. All 26 performance tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:35:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.12","title":"[track] Documentation Phase 1: Core features keybindings and runbook","description":"Update documentation to reflect TUI V2 changes:

1. Keybinding card update:
   - Add Ctrl+P: Command Palette (NOT / which conflicts with per-screen search bars)
     NOTE: Ctrl+K was DROPPED (conflicts with readline kill-to-end). Ctrl+P is the ONLY palette trigger.
   - Add . (period): Contextual action menu on selected item
   - Add Ctrl+T: Focus toast notification stack (NOT bare t which conflicts with per-screen keys)
   - Add Ctrl+S: Save filter preset (safe in raw-mode TUI; XOFF disabled)
   - Add Ctrl+L: Load filter preset
   - Add Space: Toggle item selection (batch mode)
   - Add Shift+A: Select all visible items
   - Add J (CAPITAL J): Toggle JSON tree view (NOT lowercase j which conflicts with help scroll + search nav)
   - Add Alt+Left: Back navigation (navigation stack)
   - Add Ctrl+N: Open compose panel
   - Add V (CAPITAL V): Events/Commits/Combined view toggle (Timeline screen only)
   - Add v: Enter/exit visual selection mode (batch screens)
   - Add g: Global/Local inbox toggle (Messages screen only)
   - Add n: Network graph toggle (Contacts screen only)

2. OPERATOR_RUNBOOK.md updates:
   - New section: Command Palette usage guide
   - New section: Toast notification configuration
   - New section: Filter presets

3. README.md TUI section:
   - Update keybinding table
   - Add command palette description
   - Add screenshot descriptions for new features

4. AGENTS.md TUI section:
   - Update screen count and descriptions
   - Add new env vars for toast configuration

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"br-2bbt.13","title":"[track] Agent Communication Network Graph Visualization","description":"## Rationale (from webapp parity)

The webapp at GET /mail/archive/network features an interactive vis.js network graph showing:
- Nodes (agents) with scaling based on message volume
- Edges (message flows) with directional arrows
- Physics-based layout
- Hover tooltips with sent/received counts

The TUI has a Contacts screen (#11) but shows a flat table, not a graph.

## Widget Reference
- ftui_extras::canvas (Braille/HalfBlock/Block modes)
- NOTE: Canvas is FEATURE-GATED behind the "canvas" feature flag in ftui_extras.
  Must enable this feature in Cargo.toml: ftui_extras = { features = ["canvas"] }
- Painter API provides: draw_line, draw_circle, draw_rect, draw_text

## Implementation Approach

1. Add a Network sub-view to the Contacts screen (toggle with n key):
   - Use ftui_extras::canvas with Braille mode for highest resolution
   - Draw agent nodes as circles with size proportional to message count
   - Draw directed edges (arrows) between communicating agents
   - Edge thickness proportional to message volume between pair
   - Color coding: green=active contact, yellow=pending, red=blocked

2. Data source: query agent_links + message counts from DB
   - Cache graph layout (recompute only on data change)
   - Simple force-directed layout (or fixed grid for <20 agents)

3. Interaction:
   - Arrow keys to select/highlight a node
   - Enter on node to deep link to Agents screen (via br-2bbt.8)
   - Display tooltip with sent/received counts near selected node
   - Zoom in/out with +/- keys (changes canvas scale)

4. Fallback: At Tiny terminal size, show tabular view instead of graph

## Acceptance Criteria
- Network graph renders agent communication patterns visually
- Node sizing reflects message volume
- Edge directionality shows message flow
- Works at Compact and Large terminal sizes (Tiny falls back to table)
- Canvas feature flag enabled in Cargo.toml
- Unit tests for layout algorithm and data aggregation
- Snapshot tests for graph rendering with sample data","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)
